page: Microsoft Azure NC32ads_A10_v4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCADSA10v4 family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD x86_64 processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 400.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.5 GB per vCPU.

This server includes a single NVIDIA A10 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. For local storage, the instance is provisioned with 2949 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput storage configurations. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the integration of a dedicated NVIDIA A10 GPU, substantial system memory, and large local storage, this instance offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring both high-capacity RAM and hardware acceleration. It is designed for workloads such as GPU-accelerated databases, deep learning inference, data analytics, and memory-intensive graphics processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure NC32ads_A10_v4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCADSA10v4 family, powered by an x86_64 AMD processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs. It features 400.0 GB of system memory, translating to 12.5 GB per vCPU, and is equipped with a single NVIDIA A10 GPU containing 22 GB of VRAM. Storage needs are met by 2949 GB of local disk, with Premium Storage capability supported. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware configuration delivers a balanced ratio of compute, memory, and graphics acceleration without compromising local storage performance. The combination of high-capacity system memory, dedicated GPU acceleration, and substantial local storage makes this instance well-suited for demanding workloads such as data science, machine learning inference, and memory-intensive database operations.
